mapping.a_element: the stochastic exclusion/driven-diffusive process whose stationary state is sought
mapping.b_element: the Markov chain on a graph whose transition probabilities are being set

mapping.a_element: the matrices of the matrix-product ansatz encoding the steady-state microstate distribution
mapping.b_element: the transition-probability matrix whose second-largest eigenvalue modulus is minimized

brief_justification: Both concern Markov/stochastic processes and use matrices, but the machinery differs: paper A is an algebraic matrix-product-state ansatz (a non-commutative-algebra representation) for computing the stationary distribution of an exclusion process, whereas paper B is a semidefinite-programming optimization of a chain's transition matrix that minimizes its spectral gap (SLEM) using representation-theoretic block-diagonalization of graph symmetries. The matrices play opposite roles (state-distribution representation vs. the transition operator being optimized), so no coherent role-for-role mapping of the driving method holds.
